volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 5.2.931 - Section 5.4.979)

sections.label: Section 5.2.931
sections.original: iam in currum escendi , iam lora ín manus cepi meas .
sections.translation: Now I ’ ve ascended the chariot ; now I ’ ve taken the reins in my hands .

sections.label: Section 5.2.932
sections.translation: You are not in your senses .

sections.label: Section 5.2.932b
sections.original: Quin , pedes , vos in curriculum conicitis
sections.translation: Feet of mine , why don ’ t ye betake yourselves into the chariot , straight for Cyprus , since my father determines on my banishment ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.934
sections.original: Stultus es , noli istuc quaeso dicere .
sections.translation: You are silly . Prithee , don ’ t be saying this .

sections.label: Section 5.2.934b
sections.translation: I ’ m resolved to persist —

sections.label: Section 5.2.935
sections.original: operam ut sumam ad pervestigandum , ubi sit illaec .
sections.translation: to use my endeavours to seek her out where she is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.935b
sections.translation: Why , she ’ s at our house .

sections.label: Section 5.2.936
sections.original: Nam hic quod dixit , id mentitust .
sections.translation: For what that person said , he told a falsehood in it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.936b
sections.translation: Really , I told you the truth .

sections.label: Section 5.2.937
sections.translation: Now I ’ ve come to Cyprus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.937b
sections.original: Quin sequere , ut illam videas quam expetis .
sections.translation: Nay , but follow me , that you may see her whom you are looking for .

sections.label: Section 5.2.938
sections.translation: Enquiring there , I didn ’ t find her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.938b
sections.translation: I ’ ll not care then for my mother ’ s anger .

sections.label: Section 5.2.939
sections.original: Porro proficiscor quaesitum . nunc perveni Chalcidem ;
sections.translation: I ’ ll still go on to seek her . Now I ’ ve got to Chalcis ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.940
sections.original: video ibi hospitem Zacyntho , dico quid eo advenerim ,
sections.translation: I see there my former host at Zacynthus ; I tell him why I ’ ve come thither ; I make enquiry if he has heard say who has brought her thither , who has got possession of her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.942
sections.original: Quin tu istas omittis nugas ac mecum huc intro ambulas ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you cease that nonsense , and step with me this way in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.943
sections.original: Hospes respondit , Zacynthi ficos fieri non malas .
sections.translation: My host answered that figs grew , not bad ones , at Zacynthus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.944
sections.translation: He didn ’ t say false there .

sections.label: Section 5.2.944b
sections.original: Sed de amica se indaudivisse autumat ,
sections.translation: But he says that he has heard about my mistress , that she ’ s here at Athens .

sections.label: Section 5.2.945b
sections.translation: Really , this Zacynthian is quite a Calchas [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.946
sections.original: Navem conscendo , proficiscor ilico . iam sum domi ,
sections.translation: I get aboard ship , and start at once . I ’ m now at home ; now I ’ ve returned from banishment . My friend , Eutychus ( turning towards him ) , greetings to you ! How have you been ? How are my parents ? Are they well ? Do you come to my mother , you say — you invite me kindly ; you speak politely . At your house to-morrow ; for the present at home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.950
sections.translation: So it is proper ; so it ought to be done .

sections.label: Section 5.2.950b
sections.translation: How now ? What are you dreaming about ? This man ’ s not in his senses .

sections.label: Section 5.2.951b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you , as a friend , make haste to cure me then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.952
sections.translation: Follow me , please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.952b
sections.translation: I ’ m following .

sections.label: Section 5.2.952c
sections.translation: Softly , pray ; you are treading on my heels . Don ’ t you hear me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.953b
sections.translation: I ’ ve heard you for some time past .

sections.label: Section 5.2.953c
sections.translation: I want a reconciliation to be made between my father and mother ; for now she ’ s in a passion —

sections.label: Section 5.2.954b
sections.translation: Only do go on .

sections.label: Section 5.2.955
sections.translation: About that woman —

sections.label: Section 5.2.955b
sections.translation: Only do go on .

sections.label: Section 5.2.955c
sections.translation: Therefore take care —

sections.label: Section 5.2.955d
sections.translation: Nay , but do go on then ; I ’ ll make her as mild as Juno is when she ’ s kind to Jupiter .

sections.label: Section 5.3.957
sections.original: Quasi tu numquam quicquam adsimile húius facti feceris .
sections.translation: As though you yourself had never done anything like this action .

sections.label: Section 5.3.958
sections.original: Edepol numquam ; cavi ne quid facerem . vix vivo miser .
sections.translation: By heavens , never . I took care not to do anything : wretch that I am , I am scarcely alive ; for my wife is lying all in a ferment about her .

sections.label: Section 5.3.960
sections.original: At ego expurigationem habebo , ut ne suscenseat .
sections.translation: But I ’ ll undertake to clear you , so that she mayn ’ t be angry .

sections.label: Section 5.3.961
sections.original: Sequere me . sed exeuntem filium video meum .
sections.translation: Follow me — but I see my son coming out .

sections.label: Section 5.4.962
sections.original: Ad patrem ibo , ut matris iram sibi esse sedatam sciat .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go to my father , that he may know my mother ’ s wrath is appeased . I ’ ll return just now .

sections.label: Section 5.4.963b
sections.original: Placet principium . quid agis ? quid fit , Eutyche ?
sections.translation: The beginning pleases me . ( Going up to EUTYCHUS .) What are you about ? How goes it , Eutychus ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.964
sections.translation: Extremely opportunely have you both met me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.964b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.965
sections.original: Vxor tíbi placida ét placatast . cette dextras nunciam .
sections.translation: Your wife is peaceful and appeased . Give me your right hands this moment .

sections.label: Section 5.4.966
sections.translation: The Gods are favouring me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.966b
sections.translation: I bring you word that you have got no mistress .

sections.label: Section 5.4.967
sections.original: Di te perdant . quid negotist nam , quaeso , istuc ?
sections.translation: The Gods confound you . Why , prithee , what affair is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.967b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . Give your attention then , both of you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.968b
sections.translation: Well then , we are giving you our attention , both of us .

sections.label: Section 5.4.969
sections.original: Qui bono sunt genere nati , si sunt ingenio malo ,
sections.translation: Those who are born of a good family , if they are of bad tendencies ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.970
sections.original: suapte culpa genere sapiunt , genus ingenio ímprobant .
sections.translation: by their own faultiness withdraw nobleness from their rank , and disgrace their disposition .

sections.label: Section 5.4.971
sections.translation: He says what ’ s true .

sections.label: Section 5.4.971b
sections.translation: Then it ’ s to yourself he says it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.971c
sections.translation: For this reason is this the more true ; for at this time of life , it wasn ’ t just for you to take away from your son , a young man , his mistress , purchased with his own money .

sections.label: Section 5.4.974
sections.original: Quid tu ais ? Charini amicast illa ?
sections.translation: How say you ? Is she the mistress of Charinus ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.974b
sections.translation: How the rogue does dissemble .

sections.label: Section 5.4.975
sections.original: Ille quidem illam sese ancillam matri emisse dixerat .
sections.translation: Why , he said that he had bought her as a maidservant for his mother .

sections.label: Section 5.4.976
sections.original: Propterea igitur tu mercatu's (mercatus es) , novos amator , vetus puer ?
sections.translation: Was it for that reason , then , you bought her , you young lover , you old boy ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.977
sections.original: Optume hercle , perge tú , ego ádsistam hinc altrinsecus .
sections.translation: Very well said , i ’ troth ! Proceed , proceed . I ’ ll stand by him here on the other side . Let ’ s both load him well with such speeches as he ’ s worthy of .

sections.label: Section 5.4.978b
sections.translation: I ’ m done for .

sections.label: Section 5.4.979
sections.original: Filio suo qui innocenti fecit tantam iniuriam .
sections.translation: Who has done an injustice so great to his blameless son ;
